name: Baserow
tagline: Self-hosted no-code database and app builder, an open-source Airtable alternative
metaDescription: Baserow is a self-hosted, open-source no-code database with a spreadsheet interface, automations, and an API. Run it in one Docker command. MIT licensed.
overview: Baserow is an open-source platform for building databases without writing code. It pairs a spreadsheet-like interface with a real database underneath, so non-technical users can structure data while developers get an API to work against. You can run it on your own servers or use the hosted version at baserow.io.

It sits in the low-code / no-code space as an alternative to Airtable. Teams use it to organize data, build internal tools and portals, automate repetitive workflows, and create dashboards. Because it is API-first and self-hostable, you keep full control of your data and avoid vendor lock-in.
Under the hood Baserow is built with Django, Vue.js, and PostgreSQL. The non-premium features are released under the MIT license, which allows commercial and private use.
features: Spreadsheet-database hybrid for organizing data with familiar grid, kanban, and form views
Application builder to create portals and internal tools and publish them on your own domain
Built-in automations to handle repetitive workflows
REST API and OpenAPI schema, so it can be driven headlessly from your own code
Self-hosting with no storage restrictions, plus a hosted option at baserow.io

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to try Baserow is to run the official Docker image, then open it in your browser.

steps.heading: Run Baserow with Docker
steps.body: Start a single-container instance with a persistent data volume. Once it is running, open http://localhost in your browser to create your first account.

steps.heading: Other install options
steps.body: Baserow also documents installs via Helm, Docker Compose, Heroku, Render, Digital Ocean, AWS, Cloudron, and Railway. See https://baserow.io/docs/index for details.

steps.heading: Set up a development environment
steps.body: To contribute, clone the repo and bring up the dev stack with hot reloading, then visit http://localhost:3000.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/baserow/baserow.git
cd baserow

just dc-dev build --parallel
just dc-dev up -d

useCases: Replace an Airtable base with a self-hosted database you fully own and control
Build an internal tool or customer-facing portal on top of structured data and publish it on your own domain
Automate repetitive workflows and trigger actions when records change
Collect data through form views and visualize results in dashboards
